recent_lessons: The proj_min=0 flag on Karl-Anthony Towns triggered a precautionary -5% confidence reduction in the tier walk, landing at 74%. Towns played 22 minutes and scored 13 points (HIT). The proj_min=0 field appears to be a data artifact or placeholder rather than a true projection — future runs should document this field's behavior more precisely and avoid applying ad-hoc confidence penalties when the player is NOT_LISTED on the injury report and full quant data is present. A formal rule clarifying when proj_min=0 should trigger a skip vs. a penalty vs. no action would prevent inconsistent handling.

All 13 picks hit and 0 misses were recorded — no analytical failures to log. However, human auditors flagged 8 of 13 picks as 'trim' or 'manual_skip', meaning the pick card had meaningful structural concerns that happened not to manifest as misses today. The most concentrated risk cluster was the HOU@MEM picks (KD AST T2, Sengun PTS T10, Sengun AST T2) — all three were flagged for blowout + min_floor fragility. All three hit, but HOU won by only 10 (COMPETITIVE, not a full blowout), which likely preserved Sengun's minutes near his floor. Had HOU won by 25+, Sengun's 33 minutes and 14 PTS suggest the blowout risk was real but didn't trigger. The system should track whether min_floor < 24 picks survive competitive vs. actual blowout games at different rates.

The Sengun PTS T10 pick was flagged 'manual_skip' by the human auditor despite hitting 14 actual vs 10 threshold. The pick was structurally sound on the outcome but the manual_skip classification reflects legitimate pre-game concern: VOLATILE tag + min_floor 21.6 + spread_abs 13.5 is a three-factor fragility stack that the system should consider formalizing as a hard skip rule rather than leaving to human override. If the game had become a true blowout (margin ≥20), Sengun's floor would have been genuinely threatened. Consider adding: if VOLATILE=true AND min_floor < 22 AND spread_abs ≥ 13, skip PTS prop regardless of hit rate.

Five of eight 70-75% confidence picks hit (100% actual vs 72.5% expected) — a significant positive variance session. Over a small sample, this is expected noise. The system should not recalibrate confidence floors upward based on a single 100% session; the confidence band distribution (all picks clustered in 70-80% range, zero picks in 81%+) reflects appropriate spread_abs cap discipline. Maintaining the cap structure is validated — the caps are doing their job of preventing overconfidence, not suppressing good picks.

The SA vs. MIL blowout (+32 final margin) validated all three human verdicts flagging blowout risk on SA players: Wembanyama REB hit easily despite the spread, Fox PTS hit at 12 with only 21 minutes (T10 buffer was essential — T25/T20/T15 would have been vulnerable), and Castle PTS hit at 22 with 29 minutes but was correctly marked manual_skip given VOLATILE tag and fragility stack. The system's blowout-tier step-down logic (forcing T10 rather than T15+) was the key protective mechanism — reinforce this as confirmed effective.

Castle PTS manual_skip by human review proved to be the right call philosophically even though the pick hit (22 actual vs. 10 threshold) — the VOLATILE + spread_abs≥15 + min_floor<24 fragility stack should continue to be treated as a structural skip regardless of outcome, because a single missed night with Castle benched early in Q4 of a blowout would have produced a clean miss. One hit does not invalidate the skip rule.

Fox PTS trim (21 min played in a +32 blowout) is strong confirmation that the trim verdict was structurally sound — Fox as a trade acquisition on a blowout-prone team is the highest workload-management risk on the roster, and the system correctly identified this even though the pick ultimately hit on the conservative T10 threshold. The trim signal on Fox PTS in large-spread SA games should be formalized.

KD 3PM T2 missed (1 actual vs 2 threshold) in a 32-point HOU blowout win — the VOLATILE tag on a 3PM prop in a blowout-adjacent context (HOU won by 32, meaning KD's Q4 minutes were likely managed down) represents a compounding fragility stack: VOLATILE + blowout game script + 3PM prop's inherently high variance. The 8/10 hit rate at T2 (80% base minus 5% VOLATILE = 75%) was correctly computed, but the system should consider whether blowout_games hit rate for 3PM specifically provides a more conservative base than the overall split when the game develops into a blowout.

The Herro AST T2 pick was flagged for manual_skip by the human reviewer due to vs_soft=46% and blowout_games=43% — both below 50% — yet it hit (4 actual vs 2 threshold). This is a reminder that the human skip override, while well-reasoned structurally, suppressed a pick that cleared comfortably. The vs_soft split anomaly for Herro may be sample-size noise at T2; the system should track whether the skip rule on dual sub-50% splits is systematically overcautious at minimum valid thresholds.

Three picks tagged VOLATILE on 3PM (KD) and PTS (Sengun, SGA) all ran — Sengun PTS T10 hit for 36 and SGA PTS T20 hit for 30, but both human reviewers trimmed them citing VOLATILE fragility. The sole 3PM VOLATILE miss confirms the existing rule is correctly directional for shooting props specifically; the PTS VOLATILE hits at large margins above threshold suggest VOLATILE on PTS at low thresholds may be less dangerous than the rule implies, warranting split analysis of VOLATILE by prop type.
recent_reinforcements: Wembanyama REB at T8 (10/10 hit rate) hit comfortably with 15 actual vs. 8 threshold even in a SA blowout win (+32 margin) — validating the Wembanyama blowout exemption for REB props and confirming that his REB floor holds even when minutes are reduced (31 min logged). The 10/10 consistency at T8 with a soft opposing frontcourt after Kuzma's scratch proved to be sound construction.

Stephon Castle AST at T4 (9/10 hit rate) crushed at 10 actual vs. 4 threshold in a blowout context, reinforcing that Castle's primary playmaker role and AST volume are robust even when SA builds large leads early — his assist output is game-script-resilient because the offense runs through him regardless of margin. The 95% hit rate at T4 with a soft MIL defense was the correct anchor for this pick.

Julius Randle PTS + AST dual-prop structure in the MIN vs. DET matchup (spread 2.5, CLOSE game) was correctly identified as a low-blowout-risk opportunity; both legs hit (11 PTS, 4 AST vs. 10 and 2 thresholds) in a game MIN won by 22. The usage elevation rationale — Edwards and McDaniels both OUT — translated directly into production despite facing a tough DET defense (95% vs. tough confirmed valid).

The Nickeil Alexander-Walker PTS at T15 (8/10 hit rate, B2B rate 92%) delivered 27 actual vs. 15 threshold in a COMPETITIVE ATL win (+10), validating both the blowout-resilient B2B pick structure and the lineup amendment that flagged upside from Daniels and Okongwu being OUT. Usage redistribution to NAW in a soft SAC matchup was the correct read.

De'Aaron Fox PTS at T10 (9/10 hit rate) hit at 12 actual vs. 10 threshold in the SA blowout win, with only 21 minutes played — confirming the system's caution about Fox's workload management risk was warranted but the conservative T10 threshold provided sufficient buffer even with compressed minutes. The spread cap to 74% was appropriate given the minutes risk realized.

Low-threshold PTS and REB anchors (T10 and T4) with 9/10 hit rates delivered clean floors across all games — Barnes PTS T10 (23 actual), Kawhi REB T4 (8 actual), SGA AST T4 (4 actual exact), and KD REB T4 (4 actual exact) all hit, confirming that conservative tier selection at high hit rates is the session's most reliable pattern.

The IND amplifier context delivered again: Adebayo PTS T10 hit for 15, Herro PTS T10 hit for 31, and Adebayo AST T2 hit for 3 in a game IND won by 17 — even in a competitive loss for MIA, the IND soft-defense amplifier continued to boost visiting scorers' counting stats, validating the amplifier rule for offensive props.

Multi-prop stacking on a single player worked well when backed by consistent tags and low threshts: KD's four-prop stack (PTS/REB/AST/3PM) went 3/4, with the sole miss being the 3PM VOLATILE pick — the non-VOLATILE legs (REB and AST) both hit exactly at threshold, confirming that consistent-tagged low-tier props on the same player are the reliable legs of any stack.

The HOU blowout context (HOU won by 32 vs NO) did not create garbage-time compression for HOU's primary contributors: KD (29 min), Sengun (32 min), and their combined counting stats all cleared low thresholds comfortably, suggesting the HOU suppressor scheme note applies to opponents facing HOU, not HOU players themselves — this distinction should be preserved in future analysis.

The lineup amendment system correctly identified upside on Kawhi (MIL missing Turner/Kuzma/Rollins) and Jokic (Gordon out), both of which hit their PTS thresholds despite blowout caps — the amendment's upside flags on these picks were validated by the game outcomes.
recent_recommendations: Formalize the Fox PTS blowout-management rule for SA games: when SA spread_abs≥15, De'Aaron Fox PTS should be capped at T10 or below AND flagged as a trim candidate regardless of hit rate — 21 minutes in this game confirms he is the primary rest candidate when SA builds a large lead. Do not select Fox PTS at T15+ in any SA game with spread_abs≥13.

Reinforce the Wembanyama blowout exemption for REB specifically: 15 REB in 31 minutes in a +32 blowout is the clearest possible validation that his REB floor (T8, 10/10) is structurally insulated from game-script compression. The amendment flagging upside after Kuzma's scratch (revised to 83%) was directionally correct — future SA blowout games against depleted frontcourts should trigger an upward amendment on Wembanyama REB confidence toward the spread cap.

The Castle AST at T4 hitting 10 assists (2.5x threshold) in a blowout confirms that Castle's playmaking is so deeply embedded in SAS offensive structure that even garbage-time minutes produce assist volume. Consider whether the spread cap (74%) is too conservative for Castle AST in SA blowout games — the underlying 95% hit rate at T4 with 10/10 blowout_game coverage may justify a higher floor confidence when spread_abs is 15-20 specifically for AST (not PTS, where the VOLATILE tag remains a valid suppressor).

The Randle dual-prop structure (PTS + AST) in a CLOSE-spread game with key absences (Edwards, McDaniels OUT) hit cleanly at conservative thresholds (T10 PTS, T2 AST). This pattern — star player on short-handed team, low spread, tough opponent with confirmed vs_tough≥90% — is the system's cleanest construction type. Prioritize this structure in future slates when a team's primary scorer faces usage elevation from confirmed absences with spread_abs<5.

The NAW PTS pick hitting 27 actual vs. 15 threshold after the Daniels + Okongwu double-absence lineup amendment (revised to 79%) confirms that same-team dual-absence triggers are highly valuable upside signals for secondary scorers on soft-defense opponents. When two rotation players from the same team are scratched on the same day and the surviving scorer has a B2B rate≥90% at T15, treat the lineup amendment upward revision as sufficient justification to keep the pick even under blowout-spread conditions.

Add a 3PM-specific blowout game-script rule: when a 3PM pick carries VOLATILE tag AND the pre-game spread implies potential blowout (spread_abs ≥ 10 OR the player's team is a heavy favorite), apply an additional -5% confidence penalty beyond the standard VOLATILE -5%, stacking to -10% total. KD's 3PM miss today (blowout win by 32, VOLATILE tag) is the prototype — the existing VOLATILE penalty alone did not fully price the combined risk of shot-volume compression in a garbage-time fourth quarter.

Track VOLATILE tag outcomes disaggregated by prop type across the next 30 sessions: the current VOLATILE -5% flat penalty applies identically to PTS, REB, AST, and 3PM. Today's evidence (Sengun PTS T10 hit for 36, SGA PTS T20 hit for 30, KD 3PM T2 miss for 1) suggests VOLATILE is much more damaging to 3PM than to PTS. If the pattern holds, calibrate VOLATILE penalty as -3% for PTS/REB/AST and -7% for 3PM.

When vs_soft and blowout_games splits are both below 55% on the same pick, do not merely flag the pick as 'highly marginal' at the 70% floor — treat the pick as a de facto skip unless the pick value is the system's minimum valid tier (T2 for AST, T4 for REB) AND the overall hit rate is ≥80%. This would have correctly skipped Herro AST T2 today (human reviewer reached the same conclusion manually), removing the need for post-hoc human override and aligning system output with the reviewer's judgment.

For multi-prop stacks on a single player, when one leg carries VOLATILE and others do not, flag the VOLATILE leg as the designated weak leg and reduce the player's total prop count inclusion in parlays to exclude it. KD's four-prop stack today included the 3PM VOLATILE as the only miss — had this been in a parlay, it would have been the breaking leg. The system should surface a 'VOLATILE leg present — parlay exclusion recommended' annotation when building multi-prop player stacks.

The IND amplifier (100% system hit rate, n=23) is producing consistent results — Adebayo PTS, Herro PTS, and Adebayo AST all hit comfortably vs IND today. When IND is the opponent, consider whether the blowout_games penalty should be reduced or waived for visiting team props at low thresholds (T10 PTS, T2 AST), since the amplifier mechanism (soft defense, no interior anchor) applies regardless of game script. Today's game was COMPETITIVE (IND won by 17), so game script did not suppress, but the principle should be tested: the IND amplifier and blowout risk are currently treated as independent modifiers when they may partially offset.
